attributes.description: Alerts when messages sent to a queue significantly exceed messages deleted in the evaluation window, indicating consumers are falling behind.
attributes.name: [AWS SQS OTel] Consumer completion deficit

investigation_guide.blob:
## Consumer Completion Deficit

When `NumberOfMessagesDeleted` persistently trails `NumberOfMessagesSent`, the queue is accumulating work. Delete rate is the truest measure of completed processing.

### Triage Steps
1. Check consumer count, CPU, and error rates — are workers healthy and scaled?
2. Compare `NumberOfMessagesReceived` vs `NumberOfMessagesDeleted` — are messages received but not deleted (stuck in-flight)?
3. Inspect `ApproximateNumberOfMessagesNotVisible` for in-flight saturation.
4. Review recent deployments or config changes to consumer services.
5. Check for poison messages cycling through visibility timeout without deletion.

### Customisation
- Tune `sent > 10` to require minimum traffic before evaluating (avoids idle-queue noise).
- Tune `deficit > 10` to set the allowed gap between sent and deleted messages in the window.
- For bursty workloads, increase `timeWindowSize` or `alertDelay` to tolerate short spikes.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-awscloudwatchreceiver.otel-*
| WHERE attributes.Namespace == "AWS/SQS"
    AND attributes.MetricName IN ("NumberOfMessagesSent", "NumberOfMessagesDeleted")
    AND attributes.stat == "Sum"
    AND attributes.QueueName NOT LIKE "*-dlq"
| STATS
    sent = SUM(`metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/SQS/NumberOfMessagesSent`) WHERE attributes.MetricName == "NumberOfMessagesSent",
    deleted = SUM(`metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/SQS/NumberOfMessagesDeleted`) WHERE attributes.MetricName == "NumberOfMessagesDeleted"
    BY attributes.QueueName, resource.attributes.cloud.region
// Minimum traffic to avoid alerting on idle queues
| WHERE sent > 10
| EVAL deficit = sent - deleted
// Tune: sustained completion gap (messages sent but not deleted in window)
| WHERE deficit > 10
